mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and decreases in consumer prices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, which expands profit margins and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises while CPIAUCSL also rises, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying in equities over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns during supply-constrained regimes, or the signal reverses direction when global shipping indices indicate bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate wages, which consistently support positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 850 thousand units signal a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, which drags down equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where HOUST drops below the 850 threshold, invalidating the downturn signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating inflation indicate a restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL decelerates despite rate hikes, or the interaction effect fails to explain variance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels confirms broad-based economic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during stagflation periods, or the signal direction flips negative when real wages stagnate despite GDP growth.
